# Quick start

Google Colab notebook   
[GettingStarted\_v2.ipynb](https://colab.research.google.com/drive/1etDFyhvun2A7QI6nV2LF10NvskTXCYgu)

### 1\. Copy the notebook to your drive, 

### Then rename, format is Group\_xx, Example: Group\_01, Group\_02, ….

![][image1]

### 2\. Implement your solution in cells 1, 2, 3, 4

![][image2]

Rules to make things easier for the grading process:

- **Cell 1:** Your team name “Group\_xx”  
- **Cell 2**: for downloading your own database, or indexes, …  
- Using gdown for downloading files from your drive to the notebook.  
- Your file must be shared under “anyone with the link can view this file”.  
- Your shared file must exist past the grading period. If your notebook depends on your drive files and it does not exist during the grading process, you get a zero (because your notebook cannot run).  
- DO NOT connect to your own drive in the submission file  
- **Cell 3**: for pip install libraries  
- **Cell 4**: for importing libraries  
- **Cell 5**: class Solution  
- Every new method you write must be placed under this class  
- The preparation must be in \_\_init\_\_() and the answer to each address must be in process()  
- The last 2 cells: Scoring cells, will be replaced at grading time.  
- Failure to follow these steps may or may not result in a zero.

### 3\. Press Ctrl-F9 to Run all

### 4\. Your score will display below the last cell (also, there is a detailed .xlsx file for your convenience)

![][image3]

### 5\. When you are completed with your solution, click clear all outputs, then submit BOTH the .ipynb file and the .py file.

### Both .ipynb and .py file should be in a separate folder name Group\_xx (so, if your submission folder is abc123 and your group is Group\_01, then these 2 file is at:  abc123/Group\_01/Group\_01.py abc123/Group\_01/Group\_01.ipynb

![][image4]  
![][image5]

### 6\. Submit your file to BKEL

# The grading process

The next-to-last cell will be replaced with the private test.  
Then the .ipynb file (the notebook) is run from the top to bottom using Run all (Ctrl F9)  
If the notebook fails twice, **you get a zero**, otherwise it's the score on the last cell output

# Q\&A

## Does the test always contain a real address?

Yes (mostly).

- The street, ward, province combo in a address always exists in real life  
- The house number is generated to increase the difficulty

So it does NOT have the case like “Phường Vườn Lài, Thành phố Hà Nội” (because Vườn Lài is a ward in Hồ Chí Minh city)

## I found an issue in the testcase/ notebook

Please use a shared communication channel to report the issue for your classmate to know too. I will look into it ASAP.
